page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-256GB is a general-purpose virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs. The system is configured with a shared CPU allocation and a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. It includes 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a contrast between single-core and multi-threaded capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the bottom 10% and single-threaded gzip compression in the bottom 25%. However, multi-threaded execution is robust, as evidenced by a strong top 25% rating in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics. Memory performance is balanced, showing strong cached read-only bandwidth for small blocks and average bandwidth for larger uncached blocks. Redis SET operations also perform strongly, reaching 62.83 million operations per second.

Given its shared CPU allocation and high core count, this server offers a resource-dense configuration suitable for parallelizable workloads. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and static web serving, where its average to strong multi-core performance can be leveraged. However, it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or workloads requiring dedicated CPU guarantees, local NVMe storage, or GPU acceleration.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-256GB is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, though it lacks local storage, dedicated GPU accelerators, and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. While single-core benchmarks show lower-tier performance, the server excels in multi-threaded operations, achieving strong results in PassMark CPU tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations. This shared CPU instance is highly suited for parallel processing tasks, database operations, and static web serving, offering a resource-dense configuration for workloads that can leverage its high core count without requiring dedicated CPU guarantees.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-256GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and strong multi-threaded performance, making it suitable for parallel processing workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-256GB is a KVM virtual server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-core virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory, optimized for highly parallelized multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
Equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM at a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Strong multi-threaded performance in PassMark and bzip3 decompression benchmarks
High-throughput Redis SET operations reaching 62.83 million operations per second
Suitable for parallel processing, database operations, and static web serving
